title: Unable to delete profiles with the new profile system. How do I remove them?
content:
I have used the new and old profile system, and I use Firefox Nightly. On one of my profiles I am unable to delete profiles created with the new profile system.

When I use Manage profiles and click the delete icon I get about:deleteprofile but clicking the Delete button nothing happens. The display doesn't change, no message appears in the browser console, and the profile doesn't get deleted.

This is running Firefox Nightly 153.0a1. When I use Firefox 151 on the same system (different profile) the delete works fine. When I use the same version of Firefox Nightly on a different system about:deleteprofile works fine.

Both computers are running Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS. The new profiles don't show up in about:profiles. I now have several profiles I can't delete following the instructions from https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/profile-management#w_delete-a-profile . Most of them I created, closed the window, and immediately tried to delete.

Following the instructions from https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/questions/1572980 and https://bugzilla.mozilla.org/show_bug.cgi?id=2000125#c4 I looked at the toolkit.profiles.storeID preference. The preference is the same in the child profiles as in the original profile.

I tried starting the original profile in Troubleshoot Mode. That had no effect on the behavior.

title: https://hrms.devrising.in/build/login open to this not open
content:
Illustration of a fox looking at an orange warning sign. Be careful. Something doesn’t look right.

Firefox spotted a potentially serious security issue with hrms.devrising.in. Someone pretending to be the site could try to steal things like credit card info, passwords, or emails. Advanced What makes the site look dangerous?

The site is set up to allow only secure connections, but there’s a problem with the site’s certificate. It’s possible that a bad actor is trying to impersonate the site. Sites use certificates issued by a certificate authority to prove they’re really who they say they are. Firefox doesn’t trust this site because its certificate isn’t valid for hrms.devrising.in. The certificate is only valid for: isp.afd2.jiohs.net, signup.jiohs.net, frontend.afd2.jiohs.net, www.frontend.afd2.jiohs.net, acc.afd2.jiohs.net, blk.jiohs.net.

title: Firefox set a passkey on my PayPal business account didn't give me the PIN
content:
Hi! I didn't set up a passkey on PayPal Business and the best that PayPal can figure, it was done by Firefox. I don't have the PIN for this passkey and I can't set-up another passkey because my account "already has a passkey". I can't change the passkey PIN because I don't know the PIN. I'd like to know:

  • Why would Firefox set up a passkey on PayPal business for me? ( I think that should be my decision, thank you very much.)
  • Given that one was "set-up" for me; where is the passkey hidden? Any clues on that?
  • Is there anyway to get Firefox to undo the passkey (or tell me the PIN) that I didn't request so I don't have to switch to Edge just to go to PayPal Business. It's ridiculous!
  • Has this happened to anyone else or has anyone heard of this.

The work around that PayPal recommended is that I go to PayPal from another browser. I always use Firefox and sort of resent having to switch browsers for this reason just for this account. From Microsoft Edge and I can go to my PayPal business account and just sign in with my password and there is no request for a passkey PIN. I'm not setting up a passkey at this point, which would only be wrong if I use Firefox to log on. So I'll stick with switching to Edge for the time being! Maybe someone can give me a clue.

PS: I tried to get a screenshot of the pop-up related to this issue that says "requested by Firefox" but I wasn't allowed to get a screenshot from there.

title: For some starnge reason, I cannot find one of my email servers to read the emails from my customers. The name of the website is Don Cobbler, can you please help me recover the missing emails from that site?
content:
To Firefox Support, 

          I run a small, one man business out of my home. My name is Arthur Dakos, my business name is the Ardak Co..  I am s sewing company.  I had also made a website for my father, the name of the website was Don cobbler shoe supply. The email address for Don Cobbler is, Doncobbler.com, but, for some strange reason, the email reply's for Don Cobbler has disappeared, so, I have not been able to read any emails from Don Cobbler customers for almost 1 to 2 months!  I need to read the emails from the Don Cobbler customers, so I can respond back to them. It's very important! IT USED TO BE on my AOL webpage, at the very top of the page, about 1 1/2" from the top of the page!  At the top of the page, all my email servers were conveniently located in Tabs, from left to right they were :  "the Strapguy", "Morenylon@morenylon", "DonCobbler", "Adobe acrobat", "check your Frontier", "AOL news, sports", "sales tax for Van Nuys"....   A total of 7 tabs are now missing from the top of the AOL webpage?  I do have to say, "I AM NOT A GEEK"!  I wish I was.. If I was I wouldn't have to be bothering You. Can you please help me??  My phone # here is (562)693-4750, I am located in Whittier, Ca. My email address is morenylon@aol.com. Thank you in advance for your help! I need to have to be able to read the emails for Don Cobblers customers. I have been a Firefox customer for YEARS!!  I just can't explain why this happened? So, I'm reaching out to You, at Firefox..  Thanks so much for understanding! I'll be waiting to hear back from you..  Take Care!!  Art Dakos..
